subject: physics
educational_personality: curious classroom teacher; cream textbook; diagram teaches

lesson.big_idea: Force is a push or a pull. Pressure tells you how concentrated that force is on a surface — the same push feels sharper on a smaller area.

sections.title: Have You Wondered?
sections.body: Have you ever pressed a drawing pin into a board? The tip goes in easily, yet the same push with your thumb hurts. That difference is pressure.

sections.title: Using the Diagram
sections.body: The diagram shows Force → Area → Pressure. Trace each label. This visual is here so you can see why a sharp tip bites deeper.

sections.title: Concept: Force
sections.body: Force is a push or a pull. It can start motion, stop motion, or change shape. Think of force like a hand deciding what an object does next.

sections.title: Understanding Force
sections.body: Say it simply: a force is a push or a pull. Restate that in one short sentence before you continue.

sections.title: Force in Everyday Life
sections.body: Opening a door, kicking a ball, or pulling a suitcase — each one is force you can feel.

sections.title: Worked Example — Pressure
sections.body: Compare a sharp pin and a blunt eraser under the same push. The pin has a smaller area, so pressure rises and the board yields first.

sections.title: Try This — Pressure
sections.body: Point to the diagram, then explain why a sharp knife cuts more easily than a blunt one.

sections.title: Lesson Summary
sections.body: Force acts; pressure depends on force and area. You can check one home example and feel ready for revision.

sections.title: Think About It
sections.body: Where do you notice pressure at home? Write one sentence that links today's idea to something you already knew.

sections.title: Apply Your Learning
sections.body: Design one safe classroom demo that shows how area changes pressure. Keep the force the same.

diagram_package.title: Force and Pressure
diagram_package.caption: Force → Area → Pressure
diagram_package.explanation: This diagram teaches how the same force becomes higher pressure on a smaller area.
diagram_package.practice_question: Point to Area and explain what happens to pressure when area shrinks.
